steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage explains that a larger size difference between worker and drone cells forces bees to use more nonhexagonal filler cells. The graph shows western honeybee has far fewer nonhexagonal cells (0.1–0.5%) than dwarf honeybee (2.1–2.6%) or black dwarf honeybee (2.4–2.5%). The conclusion must use this data to make a logical inference.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'worker and drone cells are closer in size in western honeybee': Fewer nonhexagonal cells means a smaller size difference between worker and drone cells — exactly what the data supports. ✓
  • Choice B — 'western and black dwarf use eight-sided cells; dwarf uses seven-sided': The graph shows percentages of nonhexagonal cells, not the specific shapes used. This cannot be inferred. ✗
  • Choice C — 'western honeybee relies on more geometrical shapes': The opposite is true — western honeybee has fewer nonhexagonal cells, meaning it uses fewer geometrical shapes. ✗
  • Choice D — 'percentage of hexagonal cells is slightly lower in western honeybee': Since western honeybee has fewer nonhexagonal cells, it actually has a higher percentage of hexagonal cells, not lower. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
